hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The rule specifies that checked items are rendered with the attribute checked="" (empty string), while unchecked or disabled items are rendered with disabled="".
The first word of the info string becomes 'language-CLASS' (e.g., 'python' -> 'language-python'), and remaining info becomes 'data-meta=STRING'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: list of default extensions in the spec
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The extension features 'table' and 'strike' are the only ones referenced in the exact_values array for parsing/rendering logic.
These are the specific node identifiers that can exist as immediate children of a non-leaf node in the tree walk, excluding containers like 'paragraph' or 'document'.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_node_tree.json.description: Parsed documents expose stable node traversal and navigation order.

feature11_node_attributes.json.description: Structured document nodes expose and update attributes appropriate to their Markdown construct.

feature12_node_mutation.json.description: Structured document nodes can be inserted, appended, edited, unwrapped, and rendered back to HTML.

feature13_normalized_errors.json.description: Invalid input, unsupported options, and invalid document operations are reported with language-neutral error categories.

feature1_basic_html.json.description: Markdown inline emphasis is rendered as HTML while preserving paragraph structure.

feature2_hardbreak_html.json.description: Line breaks inside a paragraph are rendered as hard HTML breaks when hard-break rendering is requested.

feature3_smart_punctuation.json.description: Smart punctuation rendering converts straight quotes and apostrophes to typographic punctuation.

input.markdown:
"Hello," said the spider.
"'Shelob' is my name."

cases.expected_output: “Hello,” said the spider. “‘Shelob’ is my name.”

feature4_extension_html.json.description: Optional Markdown extensions alter HTML rendering only when selected.

input.markdown:
One extension:

| a   | b   |
| --- | --- |
| c   | d   |
| **x** | |

Another extension:

~~hi~~

cases.expected_output:
One extension:

| a | b | | --- | --- | | c | d | | x | |

Another extension:

~~hi~~

input.markdown:
One extension:

| a   | b   |
| --- | --- |
| c   | d   |
| **x** | |

Another extension:

~~hi~~

feature5_fenced_code_metadata.json.description: Fenced code block info strings are rendered as language class and optional metadata when requested.

feature6_footnote_html.json.description: Footnote references and definitions render as linked superscript references and a footnotes section.

cases.expected_output:
Hello1.

  1. Hey! ↩

feature7_task_list.json.description: Task-list items render disabled checkbox inputs and expose checked states.

input.markdown:
- [x] Add task list
- [ ] Define task list

cases.expected_output:
• Add task list
  • Define task list

input.markdown:
- [x] Add task list
- [ ] Define task list

feature8_plaintext.json.description: Rendered documents can be converted to plain text while preserving list and table text layout.

input.markdown:
Hi *there*!

1. I am a numeric list.
2. I continue the list.
* Suddenly, an unordered list!
* What fun!

Okay, _enough_.

| a   | b   |
| --- | --- |
| c   | d   |

cases.expected_output:
Hi there!

1.  I am a numeric list.
2.  I continue the list.

  - Suddenly, an unordered list!
  - What fun!

Okay, enough.

| a | b |
| --- | --- |
| c | d |

feature9_commonmark_roundtrip.json.description: Serializing a parsed document back to Markdown preserves equivalent HTML when parsed again.

cases.input:
Hi *there*!

1. I am a numeric list.
2. I continue the list.
* Suddenly, an unordered list!
* What fun!

Okay, _enough_.

| a   | b   |
| --- | --- |
| c   | d   |
cases.expected_output:
{"original_html":"

Hi there!

  1. I am a numeric list.
  2. I continue the list.
  • Suddenly, an unordered list!
  • What fun!

Okay, enough.

a b
c d
","serialized_markdown":"Hi *there*\! 1. I am a numeric list. 2. I continue the list. - Suddenly, an unordered list\! - What fun\! Okay, *enough*. | a | b | | --- | --- | | c | d | ","reparsed_html":"

Hi there!

  1. I am a numeric list.
  2. I continue the list.
  • Suddenly, an unordered list!
  • What fun!

Okay, enough.

a b
c d
"}

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains plain text (string) with Markdown inline emphasis
_constraints.output_rule: Render Markdown to full HTML paragraphs with trailing newline

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a plain text string containing internal newlines
_constraints.output_rule:
Render newlines inside paragraphs as
tags, preserving the newline character in output text nodes

_constraints.input_condition: Input is an object with 'markdown' field and potentially 'hard_break' flag
_constraints.output_rule: Convert straight quotes/apostrophes to typographic versions in text nodes while keeping structure

_constraints.input_condition: Input object has 'markdown' and optional 'extensions' array
_constraints.output_rule: Only enabled extensions are parsed/rendered; disabled ones remain literal text

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains fenced code blocks with info strings
_constraints.output_rule: First word becomes 'language-CLASS', remaining info becomes 'data-meta=STRING'

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains footnote reference[superscript] and definition

_constraints.output_rule: Return comma-separated list of 'checked'/'unchecked' string labels for each item in order

_constraints.input_condition: Input has 'markdown', 'view'='toggle' with count/N number of items
_constraints.output_rule: Change first N items' checkbox state, then render as HTML view
Strip inline tags, normalize lists with indentation, render tables as text grid
Parse -> Serialize to JSON string field containing 'original_html', 'serialized_markdown', 'reparsed_html'
Deep-first traversal printing node type names separated by commas and whitespace
Apply edit to tree then render result

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains invalid type, unsupported option, or conflicting flag
_constraints.output_rule: Return newline separated lines: error=type, context=error_category, selector=node_id/type

The first word of the info string becomes 'language-CLASS' (e.g., 'python' -> 'language-python'), and remaining info becomes 'data-meta=STRING'.
